prompt: Report customers who, in their store returns for a given year, accumulated a total return amount more than 20% above the average customer's total return amount at the same store. Only consider stores located in the state of Tennessee (TN) and returns made in the year 2000. Return the customer ID, ordered ascending, limited to 100 rows.

Compare weekly sales across two consecutive years. Combining web sales and catalog sales, compute the total extended sales price for each day of the week within each fiscal week. Then, for each week of year 2001, divide each day's total by the corresponding day's total of the same week one year later (2002). Return the week sequence number and the seven day-of-week ratios (Sunday through Saturday), each rounded to 2 decimals.

For all items produced by manufacturer ID 128, report total extended store sales revenue broken down by brand and by year. Only include store sales that occurred in the 11th month of the year. Order by year, then revenue descending, then brand ID; limit to 100 rows.

Find customers whose year-over-year spending grew faster through the catalog channel than through the store and web channels. For each of the store, catalog, and web channels, compute a per-customer yearly total of ((extended list price - extended wholesale cost - extended discount) + extended sales price) / 2 for the years 2001 and 2002. Return the customer ID, first name, last name, and preferred-customer flag for customers whose 2002-over-2001 growth ratio in the catalog channel exceeds their growth ratio in both the store and web channels, considering only customers with a positive year-2001 total in every channel. Order by the returned columns; limit 100 rows.

Produce a sales, returns, and profit summary across the store, catalog, and web channels for the two-week period from 2000-08-23 through 2000-09-06. For each channel and each entity within it (store, catalog page, or web site), report total sales, total returns, and net profit (profit minus returns loss). Include channel-level subtotals and an overall grand total. Order by channel then id; limit 100 rows.

List the states in which at least 10 customers purchased an item whose price was more than 20% higher than the average price of all items in that item's category. Only consider purchases made in the month corresponding to January 2001. Return each state and its customer count, ordered by count then state; limit 100 rows.

For store sales in the year 2000, report the average quantity, average list price, average coupon amount, and average sales price for each item. Restrict to sales made to male, single customers with a 'College' education level, and to sales tied to a promotion that was not advertised by email or not part of an event. Order by item; limit 100 rows.

Report each store's name and total net profit from store sales in the second quarter of 1998. Only include stores whose ZIP code's first two digits match the first two digits of a qualifying ZIP code. A ZIP qualifies if it appears in a given fixed list of ZIP codes and also contains more than 10 preferred customers. Order by store name; limit 100 rows.

For store sales, define five quantity buckets by number of items sold: 1-20, 21-40, 41-60, 61-80, and 81-100. For each bucket return a single value: if the count of store sale line items in the bucket is greater than the bucket's threshold, return the average extended discount amount; otherwise return the average net paid. The thresholds for the five buckets are 74129, 122840, 56580, 10097, and 165306 respectively. Produce one row with the five bucket values.

Count customers grouped by their demographic profile: gender, marital status, education status, purchase estimate, credit rating, dependent count, employed-dependent count, and college-dependent count. Only include customers who live in one of the counties Rush County, Toole County, Jefferson County, Dona Ana County, or La Porte County, who made a store purchase in the first four months of 2002, and who also made a web purchase or a catalog purchase in that same period. Order by all demographic columns; limit 100 rows.
